title: Google OR-Tools — CP-SAT Solver (official documentation)
credibility_notes: Authoritative for solver capabilities; not a comparative benchmark source.

title: A Survey of Automated Timetabling (Schaerf, 1999)
credibility_notes: Classic peer-reviewed survey; dated but foundational.
relevance_notes: Frames timetabling as NP-hard with established solution methods.

title: A Survey of Metaheuristic-based Techniques for University Course Timetabling (Lewis, 2008)
relevance_notes: Documents decades of non-exact methods and their niche outcomes.

title: Machine Learning for Combinatorial Optimization: a Methodological Tour d'Horizon (Bengio, Lodi & Prouvost, 2021)
relevance_notes: Maps where ML (incl. RL) plausibly augments OR.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Combinatorial Optimization: A Survey (Mazyavkina et al., 2021)
credibility_notes: Peer-reviewed survey of RL-for-CO; notes benchmarking inconsistencies.
relevance_notes: Primary evidence on RL-CO strengths and limits.

title: International Timetabling Competition 2007 (ITC2007) — curriculum-based course timetabling track
credibility_notes: Widely-used community benchmark; defines standard instances.
relevance_notes: The natural benchmark any RL-vs-solver comparison should use.

title: Bridging the Gap Between Theory and Practice in University Timetabling (McCollum, 2007)
relevance_notes: Explains why strong methods often fail to deploy.

title: Neural Combinatorial Optimization with Reinforcement Learning (Bello et al., 2016)
credibility_notes: Foundational preprint; not domain-specific; lower evidentiary weight here.

title: Attention, Learn to Solve Routing Problems! (Kool, van Hoof & Welling, 2019)
credibility_notes: Peer-reviewed; results are on routing, not timetabling.
relevance_notes: Evidence on RL generalization limits across instance distributions.

title: Illustrative vendor blog post on 'AI-driven scheduling' (SYNTHETIC example)
credibility_notes: SYNTHETIC, low-credibility anecdote included to exercise the review stage; not a real citation.
relevance_notes: Stands in for marketing claims the skeptic perspective must discount.
